sources.name: EUR-Lex + CELLAR
sources.coverage: All EU law — treaties, regulations, directives, decisions, international agreements, consolidated acts, CJEU case law, Official Journal (L + C), COM/JOIN preparatory acts; back to 1951 (ECSC); 24 languages
sources.access: SPARQL at publications.europa.eu/webapi/rdf/sparql; Cellar REST at publications.europa.eu/resource/cellar/{uuid}; legacy SOAP webservice; bulk 'Data Dump' on op.europa.eu; ELI/CELEX/ECLI URIs; `eurlex` R package and `eu_corpus_compiler` Python wrappers
sources.format: Formex XML (native OJ), Akoma Ntoso XML, XHTML, PDF/A, RDF/XML metadata
sources.license: Commission Decision 2011/833/EU on reuse. Default (2019 decision): CC BY 4.0 for editorial/summaries, CC0 1.0 for raw data/metadata. OJ texts themselves effectively public domain (Berne art. 2(4)).

sources.name: CURIA (Court of Justice of the EU)
sources.coverage: CJEU (Court of Justice, General Court, former Civil Service Tribunal 2005–2016), cases 1953–present; judgments, orders, AG opinions
sources.access: Web search only — no public REST/SPARQL directly. CJEU jurisprudence mirrored in CELLAR (resource_type='caselaw' + ECLI). CURIA→EUR-Lex hyperlinks reportedly broken for recent cases — uncertain — verify.
sources.format: HTML + PDF on CURIA; Formex/Akoma Ntoso XML + RDF metadata via CELLAR
sources.license: Public-domain judgments + CC-BY-style editorial metadata under 2011/833

sources.name: HUDOC (European Court of Human Rights)
sources.type: official (Council of Europe — NOT an EU body)
sources.coverage: ECtHR judgments, decisions, communicated cases, advisory opinions; former European Commission of Human Rights; Committee of Ministers resolutions
sources.access: Undocumented JSON endpoint used by the frontend; no official public API. Third-party: echr-extractor (PyPI), echr-opendata.eu, HF AUEB-NLP/ecthr_cases.
sources.license: RESTRICTIVE — Council of Europe asserts DB/editorial copyright; reproduction requires permission; excerpts <500 words with credit. Official conventions and CM resolutions are PD. Not openly licensed.

sources.name: Official Journal of the EU
sources.coverage: OJ L + OJ C, 1952–present; e-OJ authentic since 1 July 2013

sources.name: EDPB (European Data Protection Board)
sources.coverage: Guidelines, opinions (Art. 64), binding decisions (Art. 65, 66), Art. 60 one-stop-shop register — since 25 May 2018
sources.access: HTML + PDF; no documented API/RSS/sitemap — uncertain — verify

sources.name: European Parliament Open Data Portal
sources.coverage: Meetings, MEPs, events, declarations, plenary + committee documents, voting records, organisation (launched Q4 2022)
sources.access: REST, DCAT-AP, data dumps; profiles epvoc, eli-ep, org-ep, dcat-ep

sources.name: N-Lex
sources.coverage: Redirect gateway to all 27 national gazettes/databases
sources.access: HTML gateway — does NOT host content

sources.name: ELI (European Legislation Identifier) framework
sources.coverage: URI + metadata + sync standard (Council conclusions 2012/C 325/02, updated 2017/2019); applied by EUR-Lex and many national gazettes
